subject: American Workers Are Winning
body:
"Even long-neglected sectors like manufacturing are having a resurgence,” Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross writes. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1453190e68&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **American Workers Are Winning**

“The U.S. economy has added millions of jobs, and even long-neglected sectors like manufacturing are having a resurgence,” Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross writes in Fox Business. Economic health has rebounded from the lows of the Obama Administration.

“Among the continuous good economic news, wage growth for America’s workers has taken center stage. For the first time since 2009, year over year nominal hourly wage growth met or surpassed 3 percent under the Trump Administration.”

With inflation in check, “not only are wages growing but the slow growth in consumer prices means that American workers’ dollars are going farther.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=18bd4002a1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Health care is the only good or service in the United States that Americans buy and use without first knowing its cost — and that needs to change," healthcare policy expert Scott Atlas writes for [CNN Business](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ec48f47d40&e=3ee4985eea). “The Trump administration is trying to institute a dose of free market reform that promises to disrupt American health care. In a flurry of pro-consumer moves, improving price transparency has been at the center of the administration's plan to reduce the cost of health care through competition.”
---
|
---
| “Vice President Mike Pence said Monday that the trade agreement the Trump administration has forged with Mexico and Canada could be the ‘template for all our future trade deals’ with the European Union, Japan and China,” Chad Livengood reports in _[Crain’s Detroit Business](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3f04c88231&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Make no mistake about it, by passing the USMCA, it will strengthen the president's hand in negotiations with China,” the Vice President said.
---
|
---
| U.S. Border Patrol agents apprehended a group of nearly 200 illegal immigrants near a base in New Mexico over the weekend. “The group consisted mostly of families and unaccompanied minors from Guatemala, Honduras and El Salvador,” Danielle Wallace reports for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=eab4b5c889&e=3ee4985eea). “Large groups like these exacerbate the current immigration process further impacting our workforce, facilities, and resources,” Interim Chief Patrol Agent Gloria Chavez said. “I commend the incredible work of our Border Patrol Agents who continue to push forward through this border crisis.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cfa600f36c&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f406659845&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=d7e303c57d)

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: It's not equal justice when a kid with an ounce of pot can get thrown in jail, while a bank executive who launders money for a drug cartel can get a bonus. It's long past time for us to reform our system.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Today I'm proposing a plan to reduce incarceration and improve justice in our country. We need to change what we choose to criminalize, reform police behavior, rein in prosecutorial abuses... | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28529773/45c30650/12f9cecd4/66859381/20498876/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
Four words are etched above the Supreme Court: Equal Justice Under Law.

But today in America, there's one justice system for the rich and powerful, and another one for everybody else.

It's not equal justice when a kid with an ounce of pot can get thrown in jail, while a bank executive who launders money for a drug cartel can get a bonus. It's not equal justice when, for the exact same crimes, Black Americans are more likely than whites to be arrested, charged, wrongfully convicted, and given harsher sentences. Right now, Latinx adults are three times more likely to be incarcerated than whites. And the United States has the highest rate of incarceration in the world.

**So today I 'm proposing a plan to reduce incarceration and improve justice in our country. We need to change what we choose to criminalize, reform police behavior, rein in prosecutorial abuses, improve how we treat people when they're incarcerated, reintegrate them when they return to our communities, and more.**

**[Add your name if you agree: It 's long past time for bold, structural change to reform our criminal justice system.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28529773/45c30650/12f9cecd4/66859380/20498876/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTUk0NlBfUkxUX0NKUl9FXzIwMTkwODIwX1YxUzJfUF9UMiJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-criminal-justice-reform?source=EMI46P_RLT_CJR_E_20190820_V1S2_P_T2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Here's my plan:

**1\. Reimagine how we talk and think about public safety.** My plan focuses on transitioning away from a punitive system and investing in evidence-based approaches that address the underlying drivers of violence and crime -- tackling it at its roots, before it ever has a chance to grow.

This includes breaking the school-to-prison pipeline, reducing homelessness and housing insecurity through my housing plan, decriminalizing mental health crises, and focusing on interventions that don't require incarceration.

**2\. Change what we choose to criminalize** -- starting by repealing the 1994 crime bill. It exacerbated incarceration rates in this country by punishing people more severely for even minor infractions and limiting discretion in charging and sentencing in our judicial system. There are some sections of law, like those relating to domestic violence, that should be retained -- but the bulk of the law must go.

My plan also involves a new approach to the War on Drugs -- which has harmed communities of color -- by decriminalizing marijuana and erasing past convictions. It will also end the criminalization poverty by ending cash bail and excessive fines and fees.

**3\. Reform how the law is enforced.** My plan will fundamentally change how police work is done in America. We'll increase federal oversight capacity, establish a federal standard for the use of force, and work with Congress to pass legislation to prohibit profiling at all levels of law enforcement. And to hold police officers accountable if they violate someone's constitutional rights, we should restrict qualified immunity.

Real reform also means expanding funding for public defenders, reining in prosecutorial abuses such as coercive plea bargaining, and creating an independent unit to hold accountable prosecutors who abuse their power. We'll also expand access to justice for people who are wrongfully imprisoned by repealing overly restrictive habeas rules and make it easier to successfully challenge wrongful detention.

**4\. Fight harsh and immoral sentencing practices and reform incarceration.** As president, I'll use the pardon and clemency powers broadly to right systemic injustices -- empowering a clemency board to make recommendations directly to the White House for broad classes of potentially deserving individuals for review -- including people who would have benefited from retroactivity under the First Step Act, who are jailed under outdated or discriminatory drug laws, or those serving mandatory minimums that should be abolished.

We should support improved staffing levels and better training for corrections officers, and humane conditions for those behind bars. As president, I will ensure that incarceration meets basic human rights standards, protect special populations, and eliminate private prisons.

**5\. Set formerly incarcerated individuals up for success.** The period after release from prison can be challenging for those returning. During this critical period, they are more likely to be unemployed, more likely to be rearrested, more likely to overdose, and more likely to die. As president, I will remove barriers that hamper reentry for formerly incarcerated people who have served their time -- from the restrictions on occupational licensing, to housing, to the disenfranchisement of over 3 million returning citizens.

**In order to create real law and order and real justice in our country, we need a structural reform of the system.**

**[I 'm ready to tackle this crisis head on, but it's going to take a grassroots movement. Add your name to support this plan, and be in this fight.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28529773/45c30650/12f9cecd4/66859380/20498876/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTUk0NlBfUkxUX0NKUl9FXzIwMTkwODIwX1YxUzJfUF9UMiJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-criminal-justice-reform?source=EMI46P_RLT_CJR_E_20190820_V1S2_P_T2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

clean_body: Today I'm proposing a plan to reduce incarceration and improve justice in our country. We need to change what we choose to criminalize, reform police behavior, rein in prosecutorial abuses... | | | "" Four words are etched above the Supreme Court: Equal Justice Under Law. But today in America, there's one justice system for the rich and powerful, and another one for everybody else. It's not equal justice when a kid with an ounce of pot can get thrown in jail, while a bank executive who launders money for a drug cartel can get a bonus. It's not equal justice when, for the exact same crimes, Black Americans are more likely than whites to be arrested, charged, wrongfully convicted, and given harsher sentences. Right now, Latinx adults are three times more likely to be incarcerated than whites. And the United States has the highest rate of incarceration in the world. So today I 'm proposing a plan to reduce incarceration and improve justice in our country. We need to change what we choose to criminalize, reform police behavior, rein in prosecutorial abuses, improve how we treat people when they're incarcerated, reintegrate them when they return to our communities, and more. "" Here's my plan: 1\. Reimagine how we talk and think about public safety. My plan focuses on transitioning away from a punitive system and investing in evidence-based approaches that address the underlying drivers of violence and crime -- tackling it at its roots, before it ever has a chance to grow. This includes breaking the school-to-prison pipeline, reducing homelessness and housing insecurity through my housing plan, decriminalizing mental health crises, and focusing on interventions that don't require incarceration. 2\. Change what we choose to criminalize -- starting by repealing the 1994 crime bill. It exacerbated incarceration rates in this country by punishing people more severely for even minor infractions and limiting discretion in charging and sentencing in our judicial system. There are some sections of law, like those relating to domestic violence, that should be retained -- but the bulk of the law must go. My plan also involves a new approach to the War on Drugs -- which has harmed communities of color -- by decriminalizing marijuana and erasing past convictions. It will also end the criminalization poverty by ending cash bail and excessive fines and fees. 3\. Reform how the law is enforced. My plan will fundamentally change how police work is done in America. We'll increase federal oversight capacity, establish a federal standard for the use of force, and work with Congress to pass legislation to prohibit profiling at all levels of law enforcement. And to hold police officers accountable if they violate someone's constitutional rights, we should restrict qualified immunity. Real reform also means expanding funding for public defenders, reining in prosecutorial abuses such as coercive plea bargaining, and creating an independent unit to hold accountable prosecutors who abuse their power. We'll also expand access to justice for people who are wrongfully imprisoned by repealing overly restrictive habeas rules and make it easier to successfully challenge wrongful detention. 4\. Fight harsh and immoral sentencing practices and reform incarceration. As president, I'll use the pardon and clemency powers broadly to right systemic injustices -- empowering a clemency board to make recommendations directly to the White House for broad classes of potentially deserving individuals for review -- including people who would have benefited from retroactivity under the First Step Act, who are jailed under outdated or discriminatory drug laws, or those serving mandatory minimums that should be abolished. We should support improved staffing levels and better training for corrections officers, and humane conditions for those behind bars. As president, I will ensure that incarceration meets basic human rights standards, protect special populations, and eliminate private prisons. 5\. Set formerly incarcerated individuals up for success. The period after release from prison can be challenging for those returning. During this critical period, they are more likely to be unemployed, more likely to be rearrested, more likely to overdose, and more likely to die. As president, I will remove barriers that hamper reentry for formerly incarcerated people who have served their time -- from the restrictions on occupational licensing, to housing, to the disenfranchisement of over 3 million returning citizens. In order to create real law and order and real justice in our country, we need a structural reform of the system. "" Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth | "" Elizabeth doesn't accept contributions from PACs of any kind or federally registered lobbyists. | Peter,

Julián Castro just secured his spot on the next debate stage. I'm grateful to have Julián's important voice in this race.

He becomes the 10th candidate to qualify alongside Joe Biden, Cory Booker, Pete Buttigieg, Kamala Harris, Amy Klobuchar, Beto O'Rourke, Bernie Sanders, Elizabeth Warren, and Andrew Yang.

There are now just 8 days to go until the DNC's deadline, and we're getting closer to the 130,000 donors we need to help secure my spot, too, but I'm not there yet.

[**_Can you rush a donation right now to help send me to the next round of debates? I want to be on stage alongside Julián, Joe, Cory, Pete, Kamala, Amy, Beto, Bernie, Elizabeth, and Andrew, but I can only get there with your help._**](http://links.mail5.spopessentials5.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=MjE5NzM2NDcS1&r=NTcyMjY0MzQ5NDY4S0&b=0&j=MTU2MTE3MDUzMAS2&mt=1&rt=0)

At the last round of debates, not a _single_ question was asked about reproductive rights or abortion care. That's despite having 20 candidates across 2 nights of debate for nearly 6 hours total.

That's absurd. So I went to St. Louis last weekend to hold a town hall about reproductive rights and bring attention to the abortion access crisis in Missouri and in many states across the country.

**I want to be at the next debate so I can _keep_ bringing attention to the issues the moderators won't**--because I've always taken on the fights we can't afford to lose.

It was thanks to the strength of this community that we qualified for the first round of debates. We can't let up now. I'm counting on you.

name: Teresa Tomlinson
subject: Did you see that billboard in Atlanta?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
body:
Friend,

Republicans are scared. They think this could be the election that we turn Georgia BLUE once and for all. They’re so scared, in fact, that the National Republican Senatorial Committee put up a billboard in Atlanta calling Teresa “too liberal for Georgia” – more than 14 months out from election day.

Washington Republicans know the only chance they have of saving David Perdue’s seat is by smearing me as too liberal, too radical, or as a socialist to divide people up. Because when they are trying to take away your rights, and we’re fighting to protect them, it’s clear who is on the people’s side.

For example, David Perdue is working every day to restrict women’s reproductive freedom — from supporting Georgia’s abortion ban, to introducing similar legislation in the Senate, to confirming anti-choice federal judges. And his allies in DC have the gall to call the women fighting for access to reproductive healthcare the radical ones.

So I have a few things to say when Republicans come after me for standing up for reproductive freedom:
Demanding that women be guaranteed access to an abortion as is our constitutional right isn’t too liberal for Georgia.
Standing up to patriarchal politicians and conservative Supreme Court nominees who threaten to take that right away isn’t too liberal for Georgia.

Repealing the Hyde Amendment so women on Medicaid can have equal access to an abortion isn’t too liberal for Georgia.

I’m not taking a dime of corporate PAC money in this campaign, and I need your help to fight back against Washington Republican attacks. Make a donation to our campaign right now so we can fight back and defend women’s reproductive freedom.

name: Jason Yates
subject: Miss the Webinar? Watch it Here!
body:
| | Peter, We are a little over four weeks away from Voter Registration Sunday, September 22, 2019! **Because of your overwhelming response and participation, hundreds of churches in more than 25 states across the nation are hosting a Voter Registration Drive to help their churchgoers register and commit to vote in every election!** Yesterday evening we had a webinar call with many of you who are leading the effort at your church. If you were able to join us, thank you for taking the time to tune in! We enjoyed answering your questions and connecting simultaneously with so many of you throughout the country. If you missed the webinar, you can [**watch the recorded version**](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=lROsI8Y7B9BWlZ2-cpdKXIKkeSI4MrcIhR7-Og50mNGp1m1BnOhfsGkI0o41zog1cUJOOT677uLq3MfUkJtdMA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190820com01&n=1) to learn about the resources and hear the answers to the questions others asked in order to be more equipped to lead Voter Registration Sunday at their church. **If you are hosting a drive, or even if you are not, we want to ask you to begin praying now for Voter Registration Sunday.** Pray for leaders and volunteers to advocate for biblical principles, not partisan politics. Pray for Christians who have never voted before to register and commit to vote in local, state and national elections. And lastly, pray for God to awaken the hearts of Christians who have been sitting on the sidelines. Pray that they would come to understand our duty as Christians to use the influence of our faith to advance biblical values in our communities and our nation. It’s an honor for our team to partner with you to see our values protected and God honored. [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=lROsI8Y7B9BWlZ2-cpdKXIKkeSI4MrcIhR7-Og50mNGp1m1BnOhfsGkI0o41zog1cUJOOT677uLq3MfUkJtdMA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190820com01&n=2) **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190820com01&n=3)

Friend,

I can't tell you how fired up I am to elect Jim Hood as the next governor of Mississippi.

I'm a Louisianan, born and bred, and I've been watching this race next door like a hawk. It's a clear toss-up -- all he needs are the resources to get out his message.

I've followed Jim's career for a long time, and I have seen few candidates who are more qualified to serve as governor.

Jim served for 10 years as a district attorney, and he's going on 16 years as Mississippi's attorney general. He took on BP when they ravaged the Mississippi and Louisiana coasts, he protected consumers in the wake of Hurricane Katrina, he's prosecuted the Klan, and he's locked up domestic abusers and child pornographers.

Not only that, but he's won statewide office _four times_ as AG. That's a recipe for a win if I ever saw one.

Jim can win this race, and he'll make a real difference as governor too -- by expanding health care, fixing Mississippi's roads and bridges, investing in public schools and teachers, and cleaning up corruption in their state government.

name: Amy Mesner, SaraGideon.com
subject: it's all because of you
body:
Thank you.
# [](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802dd3/4bef7a87/6872b122/287f6a4b/232191865/VEsH/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-email-template?refcode=em_header")

Hi team, it's Amy.
I wanted to send a quick note to say thank you.
In case you missed the good news, _Cook Political Report_ just moved our race from "leans Republican" to "toss-up." That's after just 8 weeks on the campaign trail!
None of this would have been possible without your help. Every person who made a donation, shared one of our Facebook posts, or told a friend about Sara's campaign should feel extremely proud.
There is a catch though:
**Now that Mitch McConnell and national Republican donors know there 's a real chance they could lose this race, they're likely preparing to write _even more_ big checks to try and save Susan Collins' campaign.**
That's why I'm reaching out to our grassroots supporters for help. You've always had our back, so I know I can count on you now:
[**Make a $3 contribution today to help us keep pace with Republican fundraising. I wouldn 't ask if it weren't truly important.**](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802dd3/4bef7a87/6872b122/287f6a4a/232191865/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xOTA4MjAtZnVsbCJ9/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-august?refcode=em190820-full")
Keeping up with their fundraising would be difficult under any circumstances, but our commitment to refuse corporate PAC money makes our jobs even harder.
It's worth it - we believe most of you are sick of the influence of corporations and big money in politics - but it means we're depending on supporters like you to help us close this gap.
So please, chip in what you can today. You can use this link:
**[Act.SaraGideon.com/August](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802dd3/4bef7a87/6872b122/287f6a4a/232191865/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xOTA4MjAtZnVsbCJ9/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-august?refcode=em190820-full")**
I believe in Sara and I believe in all of you. I know we have what it takes to win.
All my best,
Amy Mesner
Campaign Manager
****[CONTRIBUTE](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802dd3/4bef7a87/6872b122/287f6a4a/232191865/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xOTA4MjAtZnVsbCJ9/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-august?refcode=em190820-full")****

* * *
Sara Gideon for Maine
PO Box 812
South Freeport, ME 04078

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Prescription drug costs are soaring
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
---
Peter, One of my most critical fights in Congress is to help lower the price of prescription drugs. [**According to a report from just last year, some of the most common prescription drugs for seniors have soared in cost ten times the rate of inflation over the past five years. This is why we must control prescription drug prices. Will you stand in support of my efforts? >>>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/jtc8g7x2Ye_JMVJPQXilDDwaJdCAaEtxxVCDJ06-_DE=/i8K9FHgFRt-kb_QVWsSEc718G1sZ2bo7cTT2-wFfLWk=)
| | | [ SIGN THE PETITION >> ](https://act.myngp.com/el/jtc8g7x2Ye_JMVJPQXilDDwaJdCAaEtxxVCDJ06-_DE=/i8K9FHgFRt-kb_QVWsSEc718G1sZ2bo7cTT2-wFfLWk=)
---
Earlier this year, I helped introduce legislation to allow Medicare to negotiate prescription drug costs. The _Medicare Negotiation and Competitive Licensing Act_ is one way to help struggling households be able to afford their prescriptions. In addition to negotiating prices, this legislation would spur marketplace competition and drive down costs.

Peter, this issue is so critical to the people in my district and across the country. [**Will you take a moment and stand with me in support of this legislation? >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/jtc8g7x2Ye_JMVJPQXilDDwaJdCAaEtxxVCDJ06-_DE=/i8K9FHgFRt-kb_QVWsSEc718G1sZ2bo7cTT2-wFfLWk=)
| | [ SIGN THE PETITION >> ](https://act.myngp.com/el/jtc8g7x2Ye_JMVJPQXilDDwaJdCAaEtxxVCDJ06-_DE=/i8K9FHgFRt-kb_QVWsSEc718G1sZ2bo7cTT2-wFfLWk=)
---
Across Central Virginia and the United States, Americans are struggling with mounting healthcare costs, including the cost of necessary prescriptions. This is one step in our fight to help families facing these mounting costs.

Thank you,
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/jtc8g7x2Ye_JMVJPQXilDDwaJdCAaEtxxVCDJ06-_DE=/i8K9FHgFRt-kb_QVWsSEc718G1sZ2bo7cTT2-wFfLWk=)
_-Abigail_

subject: I'm not asking for money
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julian for the Future
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
| | **BREAKING: Juli an Castro makes history, secures spot in next Presidential Debates. _[I 'm so humbled. I want to personally say thank you -- and ask you to sign your name to show your support for our historic movement >>](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce86/3eb3e2fc/5f7ea829/744baa16/4007156143/VEsH/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190820_em_pet_b2_castro_all")_** | Hours ago, I hit the final poll I needed to officially qualify for the next Presidential debates. I know many folks underestimated us, but you just helped me prove them all wrong. I'll never forget it. [_**I don 't want to ask you for money today. Instead, I just want to ask for your signature. Will you add your name to show your support for our historic movement? >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce86/3eb3e2fc/5f7ea829/744baa16/4007156143/VEsE/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190820_em_pet_b2_castro_all")
---

I was never supposed to be a frontrunner in this presidential race.

I was never supposed to make the cut for the debate stage.

People who grew up where I did -- who were raised by single moms like I was -- who come from a family of immigrants like I do -- we were told to set our sights lower.

But you just helped me make history and prove them all wrong.

**I 'm now one of the few candidates who's officially secured a spot in the next Presidential Debates.**

[_**I'm asking you to add your name to show your support and help me prove that we 're in this for the long haul. >>**_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce86/3eb3e2fc/5f7ea829/744baa16/4007156143/VEsF/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190820_em_pet_b2_castro_all")

When the President attacked me and my family...

When the press didn't give me a fair shot...

When the far-right said someone from an immigrant community could never represent this country...

**You believed in me, and you had my back. You have no idea how much that means.**

Thank you for believing that a kid from the West Side of San Antonio deserves to share my vision with America. I won't let you down.

_**[_Thank you for giving me the opportunity to tell my story and earn your trust. And if you're still reading this, I hope I 've also earned your signature. So I'm humbly asking one last time: Will you sign your name before midnight? >>_](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce86/3eb3e2fc/5f7ea829/744baa16/4007156143/VEsC/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/julian-priorities-survey?source=20190820_em_pet_b2_castro_all")**_

Thank you so much,

Julian

| | | | | | | [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce86/3eb3e2fc/5f7ea829/744baa15/4007156143/VEsD/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://twitter.com/JulianCastro")
---
| | [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce86/3eb3e2fc/5f7ea829/744baa14/4007156143/VEsA/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://www.facebook.com/Julian-Castro-97458155742/")
---
| | [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce86/3eb3e2fc/5f7ea829/744baa13/4007156143/VEsB/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://www.instagram.com/juliancastrotx/")
---
| | [](julianforthefuture.com)
---
| | Paid for by Julian for the Future
---
| Contributions or gifts to Julian for the Future are not tax deductible.

No one like Julian Castro has ever been in the Oval Office. His grandmother immigrated here from Mexico when she was just 7 years old. She never got past the 4th grade, but she provided for her family by working as a maid and a housekeeper.

Now, her grandson is running for President of the United States. Against all odds, grassroots Democrats like YOU helped him qualify for the Democratic debates and sent him to the front of the pack of Democratic candidates.

We know we send you a lot of emails, but that's because we're relying on grassroots supporters to overcome our next big challenge:

Julian must be able to compete and build a movement that lasts for the long haul, and he's not taking a cent of corporate PAC money to do it. Will you be a part of this historic campaign?

**[DONATE](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce86/3eb3e2fc/5f7ea829/744baa12/4007156143/VEsO/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/julian-em?refcode=email_footer_donate&amount=25&recurring=on&recur_weekly=on")**
Again, we know we send you a lot of emails. We hope you'll stick with us long-term. If you want to stay updated but receive fewer emails, please let us know [here](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce86/3eb3e2fc/5f7ea829/744baa11/4007156143/VEsP/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails"). If you'd like to unsubscribe completely, we understand. Please unsubscribe [here](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fce86/3eb3e2fc/5f7ea829/744baa10/4007156143/VEsHBQ/?g=5-wKdTjfd5lcsCTt4S4B-7A "https://action.julianforthefuture.com/unsubscribe"), and we'll make sure to process your request.

name: Bill de Blasio
subject: Join me for a Q&A in one hour
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DE BLASIO 2020
body:
I'm excited to join Working Families Party supporters for a live Q&A in one hour.
# [](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21f56/1630b3eb/5177dbd1/5f54bae2/1432999756/VEsH/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://www.crowdcast.io/e/wfp2020-deblasio/register?utm_source=em_bdb")
Friend --
Working people have been getting a raw deal in this country for decades. For 40 years, the rich have gotten richer, and they've paid less and less in taxes. Meanwhile, everyday Americans are struggling more and more just to get by.
It cannot go on this way.
Tonight, I'm excited to join Working Families Party supporters across the country for a live Q&A to talk about how we are going to take back the White House from Donald Trump by putting _working people first_.
**[Click here to join me for a live Q&A with the Working Families Party TODAY at 6:30 p.m. ET/ 3:30 p.m. PT](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21f56/1630b3eb/5177dbd1/5f54bae2/1432999756/VEsE/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://www.crowdcast.io/e/wfp2020-deblasio/register?utm_source=em_bdb")**
When Trump ran for office four years ago, he said a lot of things to working people across the country. But he hasn't delivered on his promises -- only for his billionaire buddies.
Working families in this country deserve better -- and I know we can give it to them. We've already done so much in New York City since I was elected Mayor six years ago. We've led the nation by enacting the Green New Deal and establishing universal pre-K, a $15 minimum wage, paid sick leave, guaranteed health care for all, and so much more.
We can do the same thing for this entire country if we are bold enough to lead a movement that puts working people first. That's what this campaign is about, that's what the Working Families Party is about, and that's what **[I'm excited to talk with you about tonight](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21f56/1630b3eb/5177dbd1/5f54bae2/1432999756/VEsF/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://www.crowdcast.io/e/wfp2020-deblasio/register?utm_source=em_bdb")**.
Talk to you soon,
Bill

[JOIN NOW»](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21f56/1630b3eb/5177dbd1/5f54bae2/1432999756/VEsC/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://www.crowdcast.io/e/wfp2020-deblasio/register?utm_source=em_bdb")

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: In photos: Vice President Pence on our next great mission in space
body:
By 2024, America will be back on the Moon. But now our ambition is greater than ever before. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3de69db748&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • August 20, 2019
---
| | |
# **In photos: Vice President Pence on our next great mission in space**
---
| **Last month,** America celebrated the 50th anniversary of the 1969 Apollo 11 Moon landing, a moment that captivated the world. Today, during the National Space Council’s sixth meeting, Vice President Mike Pence outlined our next great mission in the heavens.

**“As we gather** here today, we do so recognizing that it's been 47 years since the last American set foot on the Moon. In fact, our great shuttle program, including the space shuttle Discovery behind me, was grounded nearly a decade ago,” the Vice President said at the National Air and Space Museum in Virginia.

**“And the truth is** , as all of you know, for too long America was content with low-Earth orbit, and missions focused on the Earth instead of aiming for the stars.”

**🎬** **Vice President Pence:** [“Our Moon to Mars mission is on track.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=62532ca84e&e=3ee4985eea)

**By 2024,** America will be back on the Moon. But now our ambition is greater than ever before. “This time our objective will be to establish a permanent presence on the lunar surface. And from there, we will develop the capabilities to journey to the red planet of Mars,” Vice President Pence said.

**When President Donald J. Trump** took office, one of his priorities was restoring America’s leadership in space. He started by quickly reviving the National Space Council, which had laid dormant for nearly a quarter-century. Along with that, the President gave a clear directive to send crewed missions to the Moon once again.

**The next step** was securing all the resources NASA would need. Earlier this year, President Trump signed into law NASA’s largest budget _ever_. Now, his Administration is working with Congress to secure $1.6 billion for new space exploration objectives.

**“After two and a half years** under the President’s leadership, America is leading in space once again. This President recognizes what the American people have known for more than a half a century,” Vice President Pence said. “Our security, our prosperity, and our very way of life, depend on American leadership and American leadership in space.”

[In photos: Vice President Pence speaks at the National Air and Space Museum.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bd75b09740&e=3ee4985eea)

**🎬****Watch:**[American astronauts will plant our flag on the surface of Mars](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=eeb193c674&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **President Trump welcomes leader of Romania**

**President Trump** hosted President Klaus Iohannis of Romania at the White House today, reaffirming the strong strategic partnership between our two nations. This year marks the 30th anniversary of Romania regaining its freedom and democracy from Communist rule.

**Today, Romania** is an invaluable NATO ally of the United States, meeting President Trump’s goal of dedicating at least two percent of its GDP to defense spending. Romania also provided one of the largest troop contributions to NATO’s mission in Afghanistan, in addition to supporting missions in Kosovo, Bosnia, and the Defeat-ISIS Coalition.

**Romania supports** the Trump Administration’s energy diversification efforts in Europe, which decrease European countries’ dependence on Russian energy sources. These efforts will increase investment opportunities for American companies, increase prosperity for the Romanian people, and improve energy security across all of Europe.

[President Trump is celebrating America’s strong partnership with Romania!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=45cf528b54&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## Photo of the Day

|

name: Wesley Hunt for Congress
subject: Fletcher Fails On NASA
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hunt for Congress
body:
On Friday, NASA announced that Huntsville, Alabama, not Houston, Texas, would
take the lead on developing the new lunar lander that would take mankind
Fletcher Fails On NASA
On Friday, NASA announced that Huntsville, Alabama, not Houston, Texas, would
take the lead on developing the new lunar lander that would take mankind back
to the moon in 2024. This is a huge blow to Houston.

Since announcing my candidacy for Congress, I have often talked about
leadership and how important it is for our representatives in Washington, D.C.,
to divorce themselves from petty disagreements, fruitless impeachment attempts,
and partisan politics and get back to work.

Unfortunately, Congresswoman Fletcher has failed to represent the people of
the 7th Congressional District in Houston and the values we hold dear.

Running a dishonest campaign in 2018 where she attempted to portray herself
as a moderate, Fletcher went so far as to attack then Congressman John
Culberson over his dedication to the space program.

While Senators Cruz and Cornyn, as well as Congressman Babin were publicly
fighting for the Johnson Space Center, Lizzie Fletcher was nowhere to be seen.
In a moment when Space City should be on the front lines of the 21st Century
of space flight, our city instead finds itself sitting on the sidelines.

The time has come for a major shift in our priorities. Lizzie Fletcher’s
priorities do not reflect the needs and desires of our community and this
latest failure underscores just how out of touch she is.

As your Congressman, I will take a leadership role and be unrelenting in my
pursuit of bringing new business opportunities that create jobs back to Houston
and more specifically, jobs for the aerospace engineering industry which has
been a pillar in our local economy for over 50 years.

To learn more about my mission and priorities for the 7th Congressional
District in Houston, visit my website at WesleyforTexas.com. Houston is a city
of fearless innovation that breeds limitless opportunity and we need leaders
who will promote these values in Washington.

Sincerely,
Wesley Hunt

endorsement by DOD [Department of Defense] or any service branch.

name: ALERT (via Team Baer)
subject: Mitch McConnell gone:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan for Colorado
body:
Mitch McConnell is single digits away from losing his seat.
|
---

| | We have good and bad news.
_**Here 's the good (actually, incredible) news:**_

Mitch McConnell is single digits away from losing his seat.

This is our best chance in more than three decades to oust him and flip the entire Senate for Democrats.

**_But here 's the bad news:_**

| | | | From Fireside Chats to Screenside Chats

**—**

I couldn't believe it when I saw it. Planned Parenthood was pushed out of Title X funding -- a major governmental health care program.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.?g=YCjTcDpTVzRarKyUt8_RZpA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-aug2019?refcode=em190820pptitleX-a&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=") Team,

I couldn't believe it when I saw it.

The Trump administration pushed Planned Parenthood out of Title X funding -- a major governmental health care program. Because Planned Parenthood informs patients about their full range of reproductive health care options, including abortions.

This has huge implications in our state. **In Minnesota alone, Planned Parenthood serves 90% of our Title X patients.** This domestic gag rule doesn't just affect Planned Parenthood's funding, but affects the availability of health care services to our friends and neighbors.

Planned Parenthood needs friends in the Senate now more than ever. **I am always in Planned Parenthood 's corner, and I need you to stand with me.**

body:
|
---
